methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $240M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Focused Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth that has raised $240M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises a modest peer universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private players including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo, though direct comparables at identical stage and valuation tier remain limited.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method here, uses the $240M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the next funding round. This method yields an implied valuation of $480M. The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set peers—while 8 same-sector companies exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier peers yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $408M to $552M, with a median of $480M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties center on the illiquidity discount appropriate for early-stage nuclear tech, the extent of capital intensity ahead, and the absence of sufficient direct Stage A comps in nuclear specifically. The single-method derivation underscores reliance on historical progression norms rather than peer market evidence.
